title: TinyWorld
description: Scene description for the Tiny World Builder. Renders an 8x8 grid of cells, each with a terrain (always set), an optional object 'kind', a stack count for objects, an optional forced buildingType variant for houses, and an optional fenceSide for fences. Defaults: terrain='grass', kind=null, floors=1, buildingType=null, fenceSide=null. Only emit cells that differ from the defaults — the renderer fills the rest with grass.

cells.description: Sparse list of non-default cells on the 8x8 grid (x in 0..7, z in 0..7). Each (x,z) must appear at most once.

cameraMode.description: Optional preferred camera. Defaults to current camera if omitted.

toolId.description: Optional tool to leave selected after load. Ignored if unknown.

terrain.description: Tile material. 'water' should not host kinds (no kinds float). 'dirt' is auto-applied under crops by the renderer; you may set it explicitly.

kind.description: Object placed on the cell, or null for none. House uses 'floors' and optional 'buildingType'. Fence uses 'fenceSide' to render a single side/leaf on one tile edge or centre line. Fences on perpendicular sides of a house can promote it to a castle turret and the connected fence component to a stone wall. Rock and hill are low-poly landmark props. Bridge is the only kind that may sit on water and auto-orients toward nearby path/land. Crop-type kinds (crop/corn/wheat/pumpkin/carrot/sunflower) imply terrain='dirt'.

floors.description: Object stack/intensity count. For houses it means floors: cottages plateau visually at 3; high-rises need 4+; manors read best at 1-3; towers at 2-6. For non-house props/crops it is enhancement density from repeat taps (1=normal, higher=larger/denser/more detailed). It must not be used to raise the ground.

terrainFloors.description: Ground height stack for the terrain layer only. Terrain repeat taps raise this value. Object repeat taps raise floors instead.

buildingType.description: Forces a single-cell house variant. Only meaningful when kind='house'. null = 'auto': cluster logic merges adjacent houses into linear/L/T/+/2x2 forms and auto-promotes solo 4+ floor houses to skyscrapers. cottage/manor/tower/skyscraper bypass clustering — adjacent forced-variant houses stay independent.

fenceSide.description: For fences only. n/s/e/w place one fence leaf on that tile edge. center-x and center-z place a centre-line fence. null defaults to n for fence and is ignored for other kinds.
